differential_diagnoses: Primary angiitis of the central nervous system (PACNS)
Infectious encephalitis (e.g., HHV-6 or other atypical viral)
Metastatic malignancy to the temporal lobes
next_steps: Send CSF for autoimmune encephalitis panel (neuronal surface antibodies including anti-LGI1, anti-GABA-B, anti-AMPAR, and intracellular antibodies like anti-Hu)
Perform whole-body PET-CT to search for occult malignancy (paraneoplastic workup)
Continue antiseizure therapy and optimize for temporal lobe epilepsy
Consider diagnostic trial of high-dose corticosteroids if autoimmune etiology is highly suspected
Repeat brain MRI in 2-4 weeks to assess for evolution of lesions or evidence of response to therapy
Detailed neuropsychological testing if patient stabilizes
